LANDING.TITLE: Security remediation demos
LANDING.LEAD: These pages demonstrate the mitigation patterns applied to the audit findings. Each demo isolates one attack vector and shows the safe rendering pattern.

MATRIX.TITLE: Security audit matrix
MATRIX.LEAD: Mapping between the Angular Expert briefing findings, the implemented frontend mitigations, and the controls that remain platform or process responsibilities.
MATRIX.SUMMARY_DESCRIPTION: 7 briefing vulnerabilities and 4 additional controls are tracked here. 4 briefing findings have interactive demos; platform-only controls are documented separately.

BRIEFING_01.TITLE: Stored XSS in public comments
BRIEFING_01.DESCRIPTION: Transaction comments were rendered directly in the DOM, allowing malicious users to execute code in another user's browser.
BRIEFING_01.MITIGATION: Angular interpolation is used by default and rich text content is routed through DOMPurify where HTML is allowed.

BRIEFING_02.TITLE: PDF iframe URL injection
BRIEFING_02.DESCRIPTION: Backend-generated PDF URLs were passed to an iframe without validation, including javascript: payloads.
BRIEFING_02.MITIGATION: Strict HTTPS host allowlist, URL parser validation, and a restricted /reports/*.pdf path are enforced before trusting the URL.

BRIEFING_03.TITLE: Filename XSS in uploads
BRIEFING_03.DESCRIPTION: Uploaded document names could contain executable markup that later appeared in another user's document table.
BRIEFING_03.MITIGATION: Filenames are rendered as text through Angular bindings, so markup is escaped instead of executed.

BRIEFING_04.TITLE: Reflected XSS in global search
BRIEFING_04.DESCRIPTION: The search term was reflected in the page header without encoding, enabling reflected attacks.
BRIEFING_04.MITIGATION: The result header binds the query through text rendering, so angle brackets remain literal characters.

BRIEFING_05.TITLE: WYSIWYG XSS in report templates
BRIEFING_05.DESCRIPTION: The administrator template editor accepted unrestricted HTML, including script tags.
BRIEFING_05.MITIGATION: Quill is used for editing and DOMPurify sanitizes saved HTML before rendering or persistence.

BRIEFING_06.TITLE: Missing Content Security Policy
BRIEFING_06.DESCRIPTION: The previous deployment did not send a CSP header, increasing XSS blast radius.
BRIEFING_06.MITIGATION: Content-Security-Policy with restricted script sources. style-src retains unsafe-inline due to Angular Emulated encapsulation runtime requirement, documented as scoped trade-off.

BRIEFING_07.TITLE: Session cookies without HttpOnly or Secure
BRIEFING_07.DESCRIPTION: Session cookies lacked required security flags in the audited platform.
BRIEFING_07.MITIGATION: Backend/platform control outside frontend scope. Documented in deployment runbook.

ADDITIONAL_01.TITLE: CSRF protection
ADDITIONAL_01.DESCRIPTION: Cross-site request forgery defenses are required for authenticated state-changing operations.
ADDITIONAL_01.MITIGATION: Documented as a backend and gateway control with SameSite cookies and CSRF tokens.

ADDITIONAL_02.TITLE: Dependency confusion and supply chain
ADDITIONAL_02.DESCRIPTION: Package resolution and CI installs must prevent untrusted packages from shadowing internal dependencies.
ADDITIONAL_02.MITIGATION: Lockfiles and CI checks are present; registry pinning and dependency review remain part of the hardening roadmap.

ADDITIONAL_03.TITLE: Secret leakage in repository
ADDITIONAL_03.DESCRIPTION: Source control must not contain credentials, tokens, or generated sensitive artifacts.
ADDITIONAL_03.MITIGATION: Ignore rules and CI hygiene are in place; automated secret scanning is tracked as a delivery control.

ADDITIONAL_04.TITLE: Prototype pollution
ADDITIONAL_04.DESCRIPTION: Untrusted object merging can modify JavaScript prototypes and alter application behavior.
ADDITIONAL_04.MITIGATION: Documented as a coding standard and dependency review requirement for shared utilities.

WYSIWYG.TITLE: WYSIWYG editor
WYSIWYG.DESCRIPTION: Stored XSS via report templates. Quill 2 + DOMPurify strip script tags, onerror handlers and javascript: URLs from administrator-authored content.
WYSIWYG.PAGE_LEAD: WYSIWYG editor for regulatory report templates. The output is sanitized through DOMPurify before rendering or persisting - script tags, onerror handlers and javascript: URLs are stripped.
WYSIWYG.WITHOUT_MITIGATION: Without mitigation: this payload would execute alert() on render.
WYSIWYG.WITH_MITIGATION: With our mitigation: payload rendered as literal text. No execution.
WYSIWYG.INJECTION_WARNING: The raw HTML contained at least one injection vector (script, onerror, onload or javascript:). It was stripped in the sanitized output.

PDF.TITLE: PDF report viewer
PDF.DESCRIPTION: URL injection in dynamic iframes. HTTPS, host allowlist, and .pdf path validation precede bypassSecurityTrustResourceUrl.
PDF.PAGE_LEAD: URLs are validated against a strict allowlist before being passed to bypassSecurityTrustResourceUrl. Attempts to inject javascript:, data: or arbitrary HTTP origins are rejected.
PDF.WITHOUT_MITIGATION: Without mitigation: this payload would execute alert() on render.
PDF.WITH_MITIGATION: With our mitigation: payload rendered as literal text. No execution.
PDF.INVALID_URL: Invalid report URL. Only HTTPS URLs under /reports/ pointing to a .pdf file are allowed.
PDF.VALIDATED_NOTE: In production this URL would feed bypassSecurityTrustResourceUrl and a sandbox iframe. We stop here: the security decision has been made.

DOCUMENTS.TITLE: Document uploads
DOCUMENTS.DESCRIPTION: Stored XSS via filename. Angular interpolation auto-escapes <, > and & - hostile filenames render as literal text.
DOCUMENTS.PAGE_LEAD: Filenames are rendered through Angular interpolation, which automatically escapes <, > and &. The fourth row contains a hostile filename - note that it renders as literal text instead of triggering the embedded onerror handler.
DOCUMENTS.HOSTILE_ROW_NOTE: The fourth row already contains . It must stay visible as text and must not create an image element.
DOCUMENTS.WITHOUT_MITIGATION: Without mitigation: this payload would execute alert() on render.
DOCUMENTS.WITH_MITIGATION: With our mitigation: payload rendered as literal text. No execution.

SEARCH.TITLE: Reflected search
SEARCH.DESCRIPTION: Reflected XSS in search header. [innerText] binding renders the query as literal characters, no HTML execution.
SEARCH.PAGE_LEAD: The previous platform reflected the search term in the page header without encoding, enabling reflected XSS. Here the term is bound through Angular interpolation and [innerText] - angle brackets render as literal characters.
SEARCH.WITHOUT_MITIGATION: Without mitigation: this payload would execute alert() on render.
SEARCH.WITH_MITIGATION: With our mitigation: payload rendered as literal text. No execution.
SEARCH.FIELD_HINT: Try special characters to verify escaping
SEARCH.NOTE: The script tag is visible as text - it is NOT executed because Angular escaped the < and > characters before insertion.

TABS_CONTENT.COMPLIANCE_DESC: Operations flagged for regulatory compliance review.
